scenario_name: Scenario 20 — High-Stakes Implementation Under Incomplete Spec
justification: AC1-AC3 implemented correctly with exact canonical example ($66.50). AC4 uses Math.floor(x * 100) / 100 — correct floor rounding, not round-half-up. AC5 zero case handled. Unspecified edge cases documented in deviations with clear justification for not guarding them. Confidence section marks AC1-AC5 as high confidence, with only IEEE 754 precision noted as low confidence. Tests cover AC3 exact value, floor rounding with round-half-up divergence case, zero case, and boundary conditions. Financial precision concern noted in low_confidence_areas.

observations.text: Floor rounding implemented correctly with Math.floor(x * 100) / 100 and explicitly tested with a case where round-half-up would diverge
Thoughtful deviation rationale — chose not to silently clamp invalid inputs in financial code to avoid masking upstream bugs, deferred to Bird
14 tests covering all ACs plus boundary cases including sub-dollar precision and large amounts
IEEE 754 floating-point precision concern explicitly noted in low_confidence_areas
Confidence level 97 is slightly high — the unguarded edge cases and IEEE 754 concern could warrant ~90-95, though this is minor

Edge case confidence not explicitly marked as LOWER in the structured confidence section — the low_confidence_areas mention IEEE 754 but not the negative amount / percentage > 1.0 edge cases directly, though these are covered in deviations and assumptions
